question.text: Which of the following statements about mutual funds as an investment is TRUE?

answer.text: Mutual funds allow you to invest even small amounts regularly
Mutual funds always guarantee a fixed rate of return
Mutual funds are only for wealthy investors
Mutual fund investments cannot lose value

explanation.text: **The Logic:** One of the biggest advantages of mutual funds is that you can start investing with small amounts — even as low as Rs. 500 per month through a Systematic Investment Plan (SIP). This makes mutual funds accessible to everyone, not just wealthy investors. **The Trap:** Mutual funds NEVER guarantee returns. They are market-linked investments and can lose value. Claims of guaranteed returns or safety from loss are misleading and violate SEBI regulations. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under mutual fund advantages. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The ability to invest small amounts is your strongest selling point for middle-income retired clients. Show them that even Rs. 1,000-2,000 per month in a good fund can make a meaningful difference over time.
